words.meaning_en: Travelling from place to place, typically for work
words.example_sentence: An itinerant musician travelled the countryside performing folk songs.

one_word_sub.question: A person who travels from place to place, especially for work

words.sentence: The ______ trader sold his goods in different village markets every week.

words.meaning_en: A temporary stay at a particular place
words.example_sentence: Her sojourn in Paris lasted only three weeks, but changed her life.

one_word_sub.question: A short temporary stay at a place

words.sentence: After a brief ______ in the hill station, she returned to her demanding city job.

words.meaning_en: Relating to a way of life involving moving from place to place without a fixed home
words.example_sentence: The nomadic tribes of the Sahara follow ancient migratory routes.

one_word_sub.question: Describing a lifestyle of constantly moving without a permanent home

words.sentence: The journalist lived a ______ lifestyle, spending months in different countries.

words.meaning_en: A journey to a sacred place for religious reasons
words.example_sentence: Every year, millions undertake the pilgrimage to Mecca.

one_word_sub.question: A journey undertaken to a holy or sacred place for religious devotion

words.sentence: The elderly couple completed their lifelong dream of a ______ to Varanasi.

words.meaning_en: A short journey or trip, usually for leisure or education
words.example_sentence: The class went on an excursion to the science museum.

one_word_sub.question: A short trip or outing made for pleasure or educational purposes

words.sentence: The school organised an educational ______ to the national heritage site.

words.meaning_en: To leave one's own country in order to settle permanently in another
words.example_sentence: Thousands emigrated to Canada in search of better opportunities.

one_word_sub.question: To leave one's native country permanently to settle in another

words.sentence: She decided to ______ to Australia after accepting a job offer there.

words.meaning_en: A planned route or journey, or a detailed schedule of travel
words.example_sentence: The travel agent prepared a detailed itinerary for the two-week tour.

one_word_sub.question: A detailed plan or schedule of a journey including routes and stops

words.sentence: She prepared a tight ______ to cover six cities in just ten days.

words.meaning_en: A person who travels on foot from place to place
words.example_sentence: The kind innkeeper offered shelter to a tired wayfarer.

one_word_sub.question: A traveller who journeys on foot across various places

words.sentence: The ancient poem describes a weary ______ seeking rest under a banyan tree.

words.meaning_en: To travel across or through an area
words.example_sentence: The expedition team traversed the Himalayan range in winter.

one_word_sub.question: To travel or move across a wide or difficult stretch of land or water

words.sentence: The cyclists planned to ______ the entire length of the coastal highway.

words.meaning_en: A long and winding journey, especially one made on foot
words.example_sentence: The monk's peregrination across India lasted several years.

one_word_sub.question: A long journey or pilgrimage, especially on foot across many places

words.sentence: His ______ through remote tribal regions gave him rare insights into indigenous cultures.
